publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-08-12 23:59:24 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Sketches, notes, manuscripts, flyers, designs, photographs, publicity and marketing materials, and other ephemera documenting Bobbito's books, films, and other projects, featuring his work as DJ, filmmaker, author, and publisher, basketball player and coach, media figure. The materials also includes copies of his books, CDs, films, and video games, and a full run of the quarterly magazine Bounce: From the Playground (32 issues), of which he served as editor-in-chief starting in 2003.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Garcia, aka Bobbito, DJ Cucumber Slice, and Kool Bob Love, was born in New York City in 1966. He is an American DJ, radio host, author, film maker, and former professional basketball player in Puerto Rico.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert "Bobbito" Garcia papers, #8160. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magazines and booklets, chiefly on basketball, and many of which featuring articles by and about Garcia. Also included are 28 issues of Bounce Magazine: for the Playground, a quarterly magazine created by Garcia devoted to streetball, particuarly in New York City. In addition to serving as an editor, Garcia also served as writer and photographer for the magazine. While most items contain features on or written by Garcia, others served as references for his work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Garcia featured as a model.

did.unittitle: La Borunqeuna Que Bonita Bandera! Written and created by Eduardo Miranda-Rodriquez

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collectible comic book and vinyl record created to commemorate the 125th anniversary of Puerto Rico's flag. Garcia featured on cover and produced the accompanying record.

did.unittitle: Que Bonita Bandera / Stretch and Bobbito and the M19sBand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vinyl record produced by Garcia that accompanied the comic book La Borunqeuna Que Bonita Bandera! Written and created by Eduardo Miranda-Rodriquez.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issues 1-28.

scopecontent.p: Garcia is the annual Boost Mobile Elite 24 HS All-American Game play-by-play announcer for ESPNU.

Wesleyan 2015-16 Men's and Women's Basketball media guide

scopecontent.p: Heavily inscribed by Garcia's classmates. Includes loose photo of basketball teams (featuring Garcia) and copy of June 6, 1984 issue of The Merionite.

did.unittitle: Series III. Photographs and basketball cards
Photo of Bobbito Garcia as an infant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Garcia's father.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some baseball cards included. Included are cards for Roberto Clemente, Orlando Cepeda, and Julius Erving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three photo albums containing pictures of, or taken by, Garcia. Included are pictures taken at Wesleyan, basketball games, film and DJ sets, performances, and other social events. Subjects include Q-Tip, Pam Grier, Rich Medina, and Rick James. Locations include NYC, Taipei, Puerto Rico
